title: Create a collection

publish.description: Name a new collection, choose how products join it, and pick the sort rule that keeps it ordered.

intro: Creating a collection in qSortBy takes three decisions: what to call it, how products join it, and how it sorts. This guide walks through each one so your new collection is live and re-ranking from day one.

steps.heading: Name the collection
steps.body: Open **Collections** from the left menu, then select **Create collection**. Give the collection a clear, shopper-facing name in the top field — this is the title customers see on your storefront. qSortBy creates a real Shopify collection in your store under this name.

els.text: Start from Collections in the left menu, then Create collection. Type a shopper-facing name — it becomes a real Shopify collection.

steps.heading: Choose how products are added
steps.body: Under **How are products added?**, pick the membership type. **Manual** lets you hand-pick each product. **Automatic** builds the collection from conditions like tag, product type, or price, so new matching products join on their own. You can always switch later.

els.text: Manual means you pick each product; automatic adds products that match your conditions.

steps.heading: Pick the sort signal
steps.body: Under **Sort products by**, choose the signal qSortBy uses to rank the collection. **Best sellers** orders by units sold; other options rank by CTR, revenue, how new a product is, inventory, or trending momentum. This is the heart of the sort rule.

els.text: Pick the ranking signal. Best sellers orders by units sold.

steps.heading: Confirm and create
steps.body: The **In plain words** panel restates your choices in a single sentence so you can sanity-check the rule before saving. When it reads the way you want, select **Create collection** to save it and start sorting.

els.text: Read the plain-words summary, then select Create collection to save.

notes.text: You can revisit the sort rule any time from the collection's Setup tab.

outro: That's a collection created: named, populated by manual or automatic membership, and wired to a sort signal. qSortBy now keeps it ordered for you.
